en: Remember also your Creator in the days of your youth, before the evil days come and the years draw near of which you will say, "I have no pleasure in them";
zh: 在你壯年的日子，當記念你的創造主。不要等到衰敗的日子，就是你所說：“我毫無喜樂”的那些年日臨到，

en: before the sun and the light and the moon and the stars are darkened and the clouds return after the rain,
zh: 日頭、光明、月亮、星宿變暗，雨後烏雲再現的時候。

en: in the day when the keepers of the house tremble, and the strong men are bent, and the grinders cease because they are few, and those who look through the windows are dimmed,
zh: 那時，看守房子的發抖，強壯的彎腰，推磨的稀少，都停了工，往窗外看的都昏暗了，

en: and the doors on the street are shut- when the sound of the grinding is low, and one rises up at the sound of a bird, and all the daughters of song are brought low-
zh: 當街的門口關閉，推磨的聲音微弱。雀鳥一叫，人就起來。唱歌的歌聲 都低微。

en: they are afraid also of what is high, and terrors are in the way; the almond tree blossoms, the grasshopper drags itself along, and desire fails, because man is going to his eternal home, and the mourners go about the streets-
zh: 人怕高處，怕路上有驚嚇。杏樹開花，蚱蜢蹣跚而行，催情果失效。因為人歸他永遠的家，哭喪的在街上遊行。

en: before the silver cord is snapped, or the golden bowl is broken, or the pitcher is shattered at the fountain, or the wheel broken at the cistern,
zh: 銀鍊斷掉，金碗破裂，瓶子在泉旁破碎，打水的輪子在井邊破爛。

en: and the dust returns to the earth as it was, and the spirit returns to God who gave it.
zh: 塵土要歸回原來之地，靈要歸回賜靈的 神。

en: Vanity of vanities, says the Preacher; all is vanity.
zh: 傳道者說：“虛空的虛空，一切都是虛空。”

en: Besides being wise, the Preacher also taught the people knowledge, weighing and studying and arranging many proverbs with great care.
zh: 傳道者不但有智慧，還將知識教訓眾人；他既揣摩，又查考，也編撰了許多箴言。

en: The Preacher sought to find words of delight, and uprightly he wrote words of truth.
zh: 傳道者搜尋可喜悅的話，正確地寫下真理。

en: The words of the wise are like goads, and like nails firmly fixed are the collected sayings; they are given by one Shepherd.
zh: 智慧人的言語像刺棒，他們所彙集的箴言像釘牢的釘子，都是一位牧者所賜的。

en: My son, beware of anything beyond these. Of making many books there is no end, and much study is a weariness of the flesh.
zh: 我兒，還有，你當受勸勉：著書多，沒有窮盡；讀書多，身體疲倦。

en: The end of the matter; all has been heard. Fear God and keep his commandments, for this is the whole duty of man.
zh: 你們一切都聽見了，總而言之，應當敬畏 神，謹守他的誡命，因為這是每一個人的本分。

en: For God will bring every deed into judgment, with every secret thing, whether good or evil.
zh: 人所作的每一件事，連一切隱藏的事，無論是善是惡， 神都必審問。
